mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 100 signals a supply-driven expansion that boosts corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4% signals a labor market tightening that compresses wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in labor market tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1,000 signals a demand-driven expansion that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in demand-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month increase in real GDP signal a monetary-labor coordinated easing that compresses discount rates and boosts wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in coordinated easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 100 and a 2-month increase in real GDP signal a supply-driven expansion that boosts corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1,000 and a 2-month increase in real GDP signal a demand-driven expansion that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in demand-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4% and a 2-month increase in real GDP signal a labor market tightening that compresses wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in labor market tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
